job_description:
About Us
We are Sila, a next-generation battery materials company. Our mission is to power the world’s transition to clean energy. To create this future, our team is building a better lithium-ion battery from the inside out today. We engineer and manufacture ground-breaking battery materials that significantly increase the energy density of batteries, while reducing their size and weight. The result? Smaller more powerful batteries that can unlock innovation in consumer devices and accelerate the mass adoption of electric cars to eliminate our dependence on fossil fuels. We're tackling one of the biggest challenges of our time every day, and together we're redefining what's possible. Are you ready to be a part of a team committed to changing the world?

Who You Are
The Vice President of Product Management is responsible for leading the strategic direction, planning, execution, and optimization of the company’s product portfolio across multiple product categories, markets, and segments. This includes oversight of Product Management, Segment, & Product Marketing ensuring cohesion across product planning, go-to-market execution, and sustained customer success.
This role demands not just executional excellence but executive-level maturity, cross-functional influence, and commercial acumen. The VP must continuously balance customer needs, competitive positioning, technical feasibility, and business performance while leading a senior team and contributing to the broader direction of the company.

Responsibilities and Duties
- Own and evolve the multi-product portfolio strategy, including expansion, sunsetting, and product line investments. Drive cross-functional alignment across RD&E, sales, marketing, service, and operations to define the roadmap, including clearly setting business cases and priorities to deliver on Sila’s strategy.
- Lead the team to develop and maintain business cases for each product line. Ensure tight alignment between product direction and business metrics: revenue growth, margin, market share, customer satisfaction. Set and track performance KPIs, including adoption, NPS, CLTV, win/loss rates, and product margin.
- Drive go-to-market planning in partnership with sales leadership. Lead initial GTM execution; then support Sales and Program leaders in scaling revenue.
- Develop differentiated segment strategies based on customer personas, vertical markets, and use-case complexity.
- Lead the integration of customer feedback loops, competitive intelligence, and usage analytics into product planning.
- Develop leaders across Product, Marketing & Segment roles.
- Guide long-term platform and technology planning with the CTO and RD&E leaders.
- Represent the company externally with customers, partners, and in public forums.
- Must have experience and knowledge of high volume manufacturing operations and concerns in order to properly represent/understand the customer and to understand the product scaling, integration, and manufacturing requirements.

Executive Leadership & Expectations
- Manages laterally and upward, not just downward—can align strong, opinionated peers and hold strategic ground.
- Able to challenge the CEO and executive team respectfully, with clarity, data, and purpose.
- Excels at distinguishing what is known vs. assumed and ensuring the organization doesn't make costly bets on opinion.
- Handles cross-departmental tension (e.g., Sales vs. R&D vs. Product) with maturity and fairness.
- Drives “straight talk” without politics, knows when to escalate, when to listen, and when to say no.
- Maintains focus and organizational cohesion during times of strategic ambiguity or market turbulence.
- Acts as the voice of the customer, not just in aggregate but through real-world, stakeholder-specific insight.

Knowledge and Skill Requirements
- Bachelor’s or advanced degree in Engineering, Physical Sciences or related technical discipline; MBA a strong plus.
- 5+ years in product management (10+ years preferred), with at least 5 years in senior leadership roles in later-stage or scaling B2B hard tech companies.
- Experience overseeing multiple product lines and interfacing with global commercial teams.
- Experience in high volume global manufacturing products; ideally in industries including consumer electronics automotive defense. Experience with customers in Asia is a strong plus.
- Deep knowledge of product lifecycle management pricing strategies segment marketing go-to-market operations.
- Proven ability to lead through influence manage cross-functional teams operate effectively at executive level.
- Must have solid technical pedigree to be able to interact with customers (operational/technical ones included) with engineering other technical personnel in company.
- Roadmaps must be aligned to economic technical realities of product market.

Physical Demands & Working Conditions
- Move items up to 15 lbs with assistance of lift equipment carts.
- Move long distances (such as from building to building) be stationary for extended periods of time.
- Operate computer other office equipment such as laptop copier/printer etc in fixed location.

Compensation
The starting base pay for this role is between $271440 340000 at time of posting. Actual base pay depends on many factors such as education experience skills. Base pay is only one part of Sila’s competitive Total Rewards package that can include benefits perks equity. Base pay range subject to change may be modified future.

Working at Sila
We believe building diverse team at Sila helps amplify individual talents. We are equal opportunity employer committed creating inclusive environment where good ideas free come from anyone. Proud celebrate diversity all qualified applicants considered employment without regard gender race sexual orientation religion age disability national origin any other status protected by law.

About Us
We are Sila, a next-generation battery materials company. Our mission is to power the world’s transition to clean energy. To create this future, our team is building a better lithium-ion battery from the inside out today. We engineer and manufacture ground-breaking battery materials that significantly increase the energy density of batteries, while reducing their size and weight. The result? Smaller more powerful batteries that can unlock innovation in consumer devices and accelerate the mass adoption of electric cars to eliminate our dependence on fossil fuels. We're tackling one of the biggest challenges of our time every day, and together we're redefining what's possible. Are you ready to be a part of a team committed to changing the world?

Who You Are
As a Battery Product Engineer, you sit at the intersection of deep tech and market strategy. You are a "full-stack" technical contributor who translates complex customer pain points into high-performance cell prototypes. You build batteries, synthesize and analyze data, and hunt for the definitive proof that Sila’s anode material provides an advantage in new and emerging markets (e.g., ESS, data/AI centers robotaxi). You thrive when you are hands-on—designing rigorous experiments and building the batteries that prove our technology’s viability. You are as comfortable in the lab troubleshooting cell performance as you are drafting a multi-year product roadmap to navigate the "ambiguous middle" where science meets the commercial world. Driven by intellectual honesty, you follow the data wherever it leads and possess the product mindset necessary to help us deploy next-generation chemistry into the hands of global consumers.

Responsibilities and Duties
- Execute the Bench-to-Market Loop: Design, build, and test high-performance cell prototypes (stacked, pouch, cylindrical) to validate Sila’s material against specific customer requirements and emerging market use cases.
- Translate Data into Insights: Analyze complex electrochemical datasets—including cycle life, swelling, and rate capability—to identify the "there-there" and determine if a technical path meets the commercial threshold.
- Bridge Technical & Commercial Gaps: Act as the primary technical interface for external customers, synthesizing their challenges into actionable R&D experiments and sharing results that clarify the product’s value proposition.
- Own the Product Roadmap: Facilitate cross-functional collaboration between R&D, Operations, and Business Development to ensure that lab-level wins are scalable and aligned with long-term market opportunities.
- Design for Discovery: Create and carry out rigorous experimental plans to explore the boundaries of our material, proactively identifying both the "unfair advantages" and the technical limitations of Sila's technology in new applications.
- Champion the "Silazen" Way: Invest in our company values, maintaining a culture of intellectual honesty and first-principles thinking as we scale our technology to global markets.

Knowledge and Skill Requirements
- Project Leadership: Ability to carefully plan, implement, and pivot multiple projects simultaneously in a fast-paced R&D environment.
- Battery Fundamentals: A strong technical background in lithium-ion batteries is a MUST. You should be deeply familiar with cell design, electrode fabrication, and the electrochemical trade-offs (energy vs. power vs. cycle life) inherent in advanced anode materials.
- Scientific Rigor: A well-organized and data-driven approach to problem-solving and experimental design (DOE). You are driven by curiosity, attention to detail, and intellectual honesty to face inconvenient data.
- Analytical Stack: Proficiency in databases and data analysis tools; experience with JMP, Python, or SQL is preferred for synthesizing large-scale cycling and characterization datasets.
- Technical Advocacy: Exceptional communication abilities, with skill to translate complex electrochemical results into a clear "data-rich narrative" for both internal R&D teams and external customers.
- Customer-Facing Experience: Direct experience working with customers or partners to understand technical pain points and define success metrics.
- Lab Excellence: Deep understanding of lab safety best practices and experience working hands-on in a battery prototyping environment (e.g., glovebox, dry room).
- The "There-There" Instinct: Experience taking products from lab-scale validation to market-ready prototypes is a strong plus.
- Education & Experience: At least a M.S. in Materials Science, Chemical Engineering, or related field with 5+ years of experience in battery industry. Candidates with advanced degree (M.S. or Ph.D.) with significant research experience may require fewer years.

Physical Demands and Working Conditions
- Wear personal protective equipment including lab coat, gloves, safety glasses, steel toe safety shoes
- Operate computer/office equipment at fixed location
- Move long distances between buildings; be stationary for extended periods
- Pass respirator fit test if needed; wear respiratory protection
- Up to 20% domestic/international travel as needed

Compensation
Starting base pay between $151,000 - $191,000 at posting time; depends on education/experience/skills; part of competitive Total Rewards package including benefits/perks/equity; pay range subject to change.

Working at Sila
Sila values diversity; equal opportunity employer committed to inclusive environment; all qualified applicants considered without regard to protected status.
